name: link-direction-forward-asymmetric
doc: The flipped-forward no-op surface (D11: single-linking is ALWAYS the drawn direction; 'to(A)' flips the edge): a NAT'd peer single-links toward a public hub with an explicit endpoint_host. The from-node has no public endpoints, so the auto-reverse fallback was ALREADY empty — the golden pins that link_direction=forward on this shape produces rendered files byte-identical to the direction-less equivalent (suppression is a no-op here) while the field itself round-trips through the compile echo. Complements 21 (both-public), where the suppression visibly removes the reverse Endpoint line.
